ingredients.item:
The core of Vietnamese cuisine is made up of the power of five flavors, which correspond to the five elements: spicy (metal), sour (wood), bitter (fire), salty (water), and sweet (earth). Likewise, many chefs strive to feature five colors in their dishes that correspond to the gastronomic senses: sight, touch, smell, sound, and taste. This salad (gỏi ngũ sắc) is the perfect example of this meticulous philosophy. It’s so colorful and will look fantastic on your party or banquet table, but it’s also simple enough to prepare for a weeknight meal.
SKILL LEVELBasic|YIELD4 servings
4 tablespoons vegetable oil, divided
1 block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu
3½ ounces (100 g) leek (white part only), thinly sliced
ingredients.original:
The core of Vietnamese cuisine is made up of the power of five flavors, which correspond to the five elements: spicy (metal), sour (wood), bitter (fire), salty (water), and sweet (earth). Likewise, many chefs strive to feature five colors in their dishes that correspond to the gastronomic senses: sight, touch, smell, sound, and taste. This salad (gỏi ngũ sắc) is the perfect example of this meticulous philosophy. It’s so colorful and will look fantastic on your party or banquet table, but it’s also simple enough to prepare for a weeknight meal.
SKILL LEVELBasic|YIELD4 servings
4 tablespoons vegetable oil, divided
1 block (14 ounces/400 g) firm tofu
3½ ounces (100 g) leek (white part only), thinly sliced

ingredients.item: 1 cube store-bought or homemade chao
ingredients.original: 1 cube store-bought or homemade chao

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon fermented soybean sauce
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon fermented soybean sauce

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon sriracha
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon sriracha

ingredients.item: 2 teaspoons sugar
ingredients.original: 2 teaspoons sugar

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon lime juice
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon lime juice

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon sesame or olive oil
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon sesame or olive oil

ingredients.item: 1 bird's eye chili pepper, minced, plus more sliced for garnish
ingredients.original: 1 bird's eye chili pepper, minced, plus more sliced for garnish

ingredients.item: 3 ounces (90 g) banana blossom, prepped and thinly sliced (optional)
ingredients.original: 3 ounces (90 g) banana blossom, prepped and thinly sliced (optional)

ingredients.item: 3 ounces (90 g) cucumber, seeds removed and thinly sliced
ingredients.original: 3 ounces (90 g) cucumber, seeds removed and thinly sliced

ingredients.item: 3 ounces (90 g) carrot, peeled and shredded
ingredients.original: 3 ounces (90 g) carrot, peeled and shredded

ingredients.item: 3 ounces (90 g) red cabbage, shredded
ingredients.original: 3 ounces (90 g) red cabbage, shredded

ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) mint
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) mint

ingredients.item: 3 tablespoons roasted peanuts, crushed
ingredients.original: 3 tablespoons roasted peanuts, crushed

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons toasted sesame seeds
ingredients.original: 2 tablespoons toasted sesame seeds

ingredients.item: 1. In a medium skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat. When hot, add tofu and cook until golden brown on all sides (about 10 minutes). Place tofu on paper towel–lined plate to drain.
ingredients.original: 1. In a medium skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat. When hot, add tofu and cook until golden brown on all sides (about 10 minutes). Place tofu on paper towel–lined plate to drain.

ingredients.item: 2. Cut tofu into thin strips and set aside.
ingredients.original: 2. Cut tofu into thin strips and set aside.

ingredients.item: 3. In a clean skillet, heat remaining 3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, and then cook and stir sliced leek until golden brown (about 2 minutes). Using a slotted spoon, transfer leek to a paper towel–lined plate to drain.
ingredients.original: 3. In a clean skillet, heat remaining 3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, and then cook and stir sliced leek until golden brown (about 2 minutes). Using a slotted spoon, transfer leek to a paper towel–lined plate to drain.

ingredients.item: 4. In a small bowl, mash the chao into a smooth paste, and then add fermented soybean and sriracha sauces, sugar, lime juice, sesame oil, and minced chili pepper, and stir well.
ingredients.original: 4. In a small bowl, mash the chao into a smooth paste, and then add fermented soybean and sriracha sauces, sugar, lime juice, sesame oil, and minced chili pepper, and stir well.

ingredients.item: 5. Arrange banana blossom slices (if using), cucumber, carrot, cabbage, and mint on a platter. Top with tofu, and then sprinkle with fried leek, peanuts, and sesame seeds.
ingredients.original: 5. Arrange banana blossom slices (if using), cucumber, carrot, cabbage, and mint on a platter. Top with tofu, and then sprinkle with fried leek, peanuts, and sesame seeds.

ingredients.item: 6. Drizzle dressing on top, garnish with chili slices, and toss well before serving.
ingredients.original: 6. Drizzle dressing on top, garnish with chili slices, and toss well before serving.
